question: Does the generated image include a grey quadcopter drone that matches the visual characteristics of the DJI Mavic 3 reference (e.g., rotor arms, camera gimbal)?
Does the generated image include a bald eagle with a white head, yellow beak, and dark brown body/wings matching the reference?
Is a golden/amber wheat field visible at the very bottom of the frame?
Is the bald eagle positioned in the foreground on the left side of the frame?
Is the drone positioned on the right side of the frame?
Does the eagle appear significantly larger than the drone to correctly convey that the eagle is in the foreground and the drone is farther away?
Does the perspective of the image appear to be a low-angle shot looking upward from the ground (showing the undersides of the flying subjects)?
Has the wheat field been correctly transformed from the reference top-down view to a distant ground plane or horizon view appropriate for an upward-looking shot?
Is the background primarily depicted as a blue sky with white clouds?
Does the drone appear to be tracking or following the eagle (oriented towards the eagle or moving in a consistent direction)?
Is the eagle free from severe anatomical errors (e.g., malformed wings, extra limbs, or distorted head)?
Is the drone free from structural distortions (e.g., missing rotors, warped arms, or melted geometry)?
Is the lighting consistent across the eagle, drone, and sky (matching color temperature and light direction)?
Is the image free from obvious compositing artifacts, unnatural halos, or text overlays?
